description_long: This stone block is a roughly hewn, irregularly shaped piece of rock with a predominantly gray and off-white coloration, interspersed with darker mineral veins and patches. Its surfaces are uneven and jagged, with visible striations and natural textural variations that suggest it was broken from a larger mass rather than cut with precision. The block has a substantial, solid appearance, and its mass is accentuated by the dense, granular stone material. One side features a handwritten marking or code in black ink, indicating it may have been cataloged or identified for a specific use. The edges and corners are mostly blunt but not perfectly squared, contributing to its rugged, utilitarian look. This type of stone block is commonly used in urban landscaping, retaining walls, or as a barrier in public spaces.
description: A rugged, irregular stone block with gray and off-white tones, marked by dark mineral veins and a handwritten code on one side. Its rough, natural texture and substantial mass make it suitable for use as an urban barrier or landscaping element.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the stone block, featuring prominent gray striations and a visible handwritten marking in black ink.
description_view_1: This angle highlights a more mottled, yellowish-brown surface with less visible striation and no markings, emphasizing the block's rough, natural texture.
description_view_2: Here, the block's irregular side is visible, with a mix of gray and off-white tones and pronounced mineral banding.
description_view_3: This view displays a relatively flat, rectangular face with subtle gray veining and a more uniform coloration, lacking any visible markings.
attribute_weathering_pattern: Minor surface discoloration and roughness, with some edges appearing slightly chipped; no significant moss or erosion visible.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Handwritten black ink code visible on one face.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Typically isolated or loosely stacked with other blocks.
